author: tuyen4818
content:
good findings, so the lodestar fix for `shouldExtendPayload()` does not change anything regarding this issue, everybody  votes for 22903 FULL anyway
`should_build_on_full()` of `lodestar_ethrex_2` for sure returning false in this case due to majority of ptc members voted for false (block 22929, 22930)
`should_build_on_full()` of `lodestar_ethrex_1` returns true (check block 22907), I think ptc is not fully spread and each node has its own view

author: tuyen4818
content:
I'm copying the weight of 22903 I found in lodestar nodes (others can double check on your nodes)

```
lodestar_nethermind_2
EMPTY:    9504
FULL:    15968

lodestar_ethrex_1
EMPTY:    6944
FULL:    19904

lodestar_ethrex_2
EMPTY:    9216
FULL:    14848
```

it's clear that we should not build on 22903 EMPTY in this case, even through `should_build_on_full()` returning false, especially with multiple skipped slots
note that head is 22903 FULL, if we build on 22903 EMPTY, we are doing proposer boost reorg in a sense
and we should not do it in the case of skipped slots because 22903 FULL is super strong, proposer weight (of ~960) does not help
I believe this is the same reason why we don't do proposer boost reorg when there is skipped slot https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/5898db97eccadd3407e1216035c831bca559d2fe/specs/phase0/fork-choice.md?plain=1#L685 cc <@602753420033785856> if you can confirm

author: tuyen4818
content:
so when `should_build_on_full()` is against our head, we should follow some conditions in proposer boost reorg
at least the skipped slot condition
